description: optimizerDuck utilizes a layered MVVM architecture designed for extensible Windows system optimization. The UI Presentation Layer captures user intent and binds to the ViewModel Orchestration Layer, which manages application state and triggers the Optimization Core Engine. This central engine discovers and executes System Tweaks & Feature Modules while coordinating with the Windows Interop & Revert Provider to perform low-level system changes and maintain a persistent state for safe reversibility.

components.description: Contains the primary user interface screens and category-specific layouts where users interact with optimization and customization modules.

components.name: Presentation Infrastructure & Shell Services
components.description: Provides the foundational UI controls, navigation logic, and services that interact directly with the Windows Shell to refresh the system environment.

components.name: XAML Binding & Localization Support
components.description: Manages the transformation of ViewModel data into UI-ready formats and handles the dynamic injection of localized strings into XAML elements.

components_relations.relation: Uses markup extensions and converters to bind ViewModel properties to localized UI elements.
Triggers shell refreshes via SystemRefreshService after applying system-wide optimizations.
Provides the NavigationView framework that hosts and navigates between different feature pages.

components.name: ViewModel Orchestration Layer
components.description: Bridges the UI and the Engine, managing application state, user commands, and the mapping of optimization categories to the view.

components.name: Main Window Orchestrator
components.description: The primary entry point for the UI layer, managing top-level state, application initialization, and navigation between views.

components.name: Optimization View Mapping
components.description: A translation layer converting technical optimization categories and system tags into interactive ViewModels for the UI.

components.name: Application State Management
components.description: Manages persistent configuration and domain models, handling serialization of user preferences and providing observable data structures.

components_relations.relation: Aggregates and initializes category ViewModels during application startup.
Loads global AppSettings to configure the UI environment and user preferences.
Binds UI elements to specific configuration properties to reflect current system state.
Notifies the orchestrator of configuration changes that require UI-wide refreshes.

components.name: Optimization Core Engine
components.description: The central controller that manages the lifecycle of optimizations, coordinates revert operations, and manages execution scopes.

components.name: System Tweaks & Feature Modules
components.description: Contains the actual logic for system tweaks and specialized utilities like bloatware removal and disk cleanup.

components.name: Privacy & Security Hardening
components.description: Manages the reduction of the Windows attack surface and data collection footprint, targeting telemetry services, AI assistants, and background activity logging.

components.name: Performance & Resource Optimization
components.description: Consolidates modules aimed at improving system responsiveness and resource utilization by managing background process priorities, power plans, and non-essential services.

components.name: Desktop & Gaming Customization
components.description: Focuses on user environment tailoring, specifically modifying the Windows Shell and optimizing system parameters for low-latency gaming and visual clarity.

components.name: Specialized Maintenance Services
